Lock.SetupBlurb: Your entries are stored only on this device, encrypted. Lock them behind Windows Hello and/or a PIN. (AI replies — and, until you add local embeddings, the text sent for semantic recall — go to the provider you choose; see the masking option below.)
Lock.HelloUnavailable: Windows Hello isn't available here — set a PIN.
Lock.PinPlaceholder: Passcode (min 6 — a letters+numbers passphrase is far stronger)
Lock.MaskConsentTitle: Mask contact details before they leave this device
Lock.MaskConsentDetail: Emails, phone numbers, and ID numbers are swapped for placeholders before text is sent to the AI. Names are not masked yet — and free text can still identify you, so treat this as harm-reduction, not anonymity. For full privacy, use a local model (⋯ → LLM settings). Changeable in Security.
Lock.LockedSub: Unlock your encrypted journal and memory vault.
Lock.PinTooShort: Set a passcode of at least 6 characters — a longer passphrase with letters is far stronger (it's the key to your whole vault).
Lock.LockedOut: Too many wrong attempts — locked for {0} seconds.
Menu.PredictionScoresTooltip: How the analyst's predictions scored against what actually happened.
Menu.CheckinTooltip: A daily one-line invitation to reflect, at this hour — skipped on days you already wrote. Click to cycle.
Menu.DiscreetTooltip: Discreet: every Windows notification shows a generic line — nothing personal on the lock screen, ever.
Menu.ResetSandboxTooltip: Wipe the dev sandbox and start a virgin thread (DEBUG only; never touches real data)
Menu.UpdateAvailableTooltip: Opens the GitHub releases page in your browser — nothing downloads automatically.
Menu.EgressSummaryTooltip: What actually leaves this machine right now
NotFound.Title: Not Found
NotFound.Body: Sorry, the content you are looking for does not exist.
Timeline.Title: Timeline
Timeline.Empty: Nothing yet — entries and dated events will appear here.
Ledger.Title: Calibration — track record
Ledger.Empty: No predictions yet. When you forecast something, the analyst logs a falsifiable prediction and scores it later.
Search.Title: Search entries
Search.Placeholder: Find exact text in your entries…
Search.Hint: Instant, on-device — no AI call. Click a result to jump to it.
Programs.Title: Guided programs
Programs.Intro: A short, gentle sequence of daily prompts. One a day; skip any day you like.
Import.Title: Import {0} file(s)
Import.Body: Each file is distilled by the deep analyst into your model (notes, graph, events) — standardized, deduped against what you already have. Imported content is reference, not chat.
Import.UploadWarning: This uploads file contents to OpenAI for analysis + embeddings.
Import.ResumingSkipped: resuming — {0} chunk(s) already imported, skipping them.
Import.Weaving: weaving your history into the running summary…
Import.Woven: woven {0} entries into your story so far…
Erase.Title: Erase everything?
Erase.Warning: This permanently deletes your entire thread, every note, all embeddings, and your profile from this machine. The thread is meant to be forever — this cannot be undone and there is no backup.
Export.Title: Export
Export.Blurb: Writes JSON, Markdown, CSV, Mermaid (.mmd) and GraphML to your Desktop. Leave the dates empty for everything.
Export.MaskedDetail: Emails, phone numbers and IDs are replaced with placeholders. Names are not masked, and content can still re-identify you — share thoughtfully.
Export.PdfButtonTitle: A printable book of your entries
Export.WarnMasked: masked (shareable) copy — emails/phones/IDs are pseudonymized (NAMES are not), and content can still re-identify
Export.WarnPlain: the export is a plaintext copy of your whole journal — delete it when you're done
Export.WarnOneDrive: . ⚠ This Desktop syncs to OneDrive, so it WILL upload to the cloud
Export.PdfExported: exported your journal as a PDF to {0} ({1})
Export.PdfWarnPlain: a plaintext book of your entries — delete it when you're done
Backup.Title: Backup & restore
Backup.Body: A backup is a complete copy of your database — thread, notes, graph, everything — encrypted with your current key. Keep it somewhere safe; it opens only with this vault's PIN.
Backup.RestoreInstruction: Restore replaces ALL current data with a backup file. Type RESTORE to arm it.
Backup.Success: backed up to {0} — the file is encrypted with your current key.
Backup.SuccessPlaintext: backed up to {0} — ⚠ dev/plaintext database, the backup is NOT encrypted.
Backup.ConfirmRequired: Type RESTORE to confirm — restoring replaces ALL current data with the backup.
Backup.RestoreFailed: restore failed — the previous data is untouched. ({0})
LocalEmb.Title: Local embeddings
LocalEmb.Installed: ✓ Installed — semantic recall runs 100% on this machine. Nothing is sent to OpenAI for embeddings and it costs nothing.
LocalEmb.NotInstalled: Semantic recall currently uses OpenAI embeddings (your text leaves the machine, masked if masking is on). Installing the local model (all-MiniLM-L6-v2, ~90 MB, downloaded once from huggingface.co) moves recall fully on-device and re-embeds your existing data locally.
LocalEmb.Active: ✓ local embeddings active — re-embedded {0} item(s){1}. Nothing leaves the machine for recall anymore.
LocalEmb.FailedSuffix: ({0} failed; they re-embed on next edit)
Security.Title: Security & privacy
Security.MaskTitle: Mask contact details before sending to the AI
Security.MaskDetail: Emails, phone numbers and ID numbers → placeholders. Names are not masked. Real values stay encrypted on this device.
Security.ChangePinBlurb: Change your PIN (re-wraps the same encryption key — your data is kept).
Security.NewPinTooShort: New PIN must be at least 6 digits.
Security.PinSaveFailed: Couldn't save the new PIN — after a restart the previous PIN still applies. ({0})
Llm.Title: LLM settings
Llm.Blurb: Choose your inference provider and model, see pricing, and store your API key. Keys are saved encrypted on this device.
Llm.LocalNote: Chat runs entirely on your machine via LM Studio's local server — free, no egress. Note: semantic recall still sends entries to OpenAI for embeddings (masked) until you install the local embedding model (⋯ → Local embeddings). Do that for a fully offline loop.
Llm.ModelIdPlaceholder: model id loaded in LM Studio
Llm.LocalHint: Load a model in LM Studio and start its server (Developer → Start server). Memory search still uses your OpenAI embeddings unless disabled.
Llm.ServerUpNoModel: Server is up but no model is loaded — load one in LM Studio.
